Validation: Student’s t-test vs. exact permutation test

Compiled at 2026-06-10 08:44:18 UTC

here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "88a30f86-0df6-4db8-8217-c59705f33b34")

Objective

We evaluate whether the two-sample Student’s t-test yields results sufficiently close to the exact permutation test under ideal conditions (normality, equal variance), to justify its use as a ground truth in simulation studies.

Design

  • Data: Independent, normally distributed with equal variances
  • Sample sizes per group: n ∈ {2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12}
  • Effect sizes: mu ∈ {0, 1, 3} (difference in means)
  • Repetitions: 100

We compute and compare p-values from: - Student’s t-test - Exact permutation test (all group label permutations)

Functions

Setting

Parallel Simulation

Combine results

Type I Error and Power

Type I error (H0=TRUE) and power (H0=FALSE)
n H0 type1_t type1_perm
2 FALSE 0.1866667 0.0000000
2 TRUE 0.0400000 0.0000000
4 FALSE 0.5800000 0.4800000
4 TRUE 0.0800000 0.0300000
6 FALSE 0.7500000 0.7566667
6 TRUE 0.0600000 0.0600000
8 FALSE 0.8366667 0.8300000
8 TRUE 0.0100000 0.0100000
10 FALSE 0.8533333 0.8533333
10 TRUE 0.0300000 0.0300000

p-value Comparison Plots

Boxplot: Difference in p-values

Scatterplot

Conclusion

The p-values from the t-test closely match those from the exact permutation test under the tested settings. Thus, for simulations under normality and equal variance, the t-test can reliably be used as a surrogate for the exact permutation test.
